 CHANGE RECORD  
 
When the quality of the products change s, the QuID is updated and a row is added to this table.  The 
third column specifies which sections  or sub-sections have been updated.   The fourth column should 
mention the version of the product to which the change applies.

5.0 November 
2025 
 A major update includes new light 
variables: PAR, ED380, ED412, and 
ED490. These are derived at local 
time noon using SOCA-light models 
(Renosh et al., 2023). The SOCA-
light model was enhanced in 2025 
through retraining, utilizing more 
profiles and slightly revised input 
variables. 
R. Sauzède, P.R.

KEY DEFINITIONS  
Accuracy Metrics 
The accuracy of the derived products is evaluated using a comprehensive 
suite of metrics computed on both the native and log -transformed data 
spaces to provide a complete picture of model performance. The RMSD 
is used to quantify the overall magnitude of the error in the original units, 
and the MAPD is used to assess the typical relative error; both of these 
metrics are computed directly on the non -log-transformed (true) data. 
In contrast, the slope and r 2 is used to measure the linearity and skill of 
the estimates relative to the observations, and this metric is specifically 
computed from the linear regression performed on the log-transformed 
data. 
errors 
The MULTIOBS_GLO_BIO_BGC_3D_REP_015_010 product provides the 
estimated error alongside each core biogeochemical variable (e.g., b bp, 
POC, Chl, PAR, ED380, ED412, and ED490). The errors for all variables, 
including bbp and Chl from the SOCA2024 retrieval, as well as PAR, ED380, 
ED412, and ED490 from the SOCA-light-2025 estimation, are determined 
using a consistent method: measuring the dispersion within the 
ensemble of neural network models used for the respective estim
